item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer discusses the new 'Mad Money' set at the New York Stock Exchange. Cramer's first show from the new set premieres at 6 p.m. eastern Monday.
item.title: Jim Cramer's new 'Mad Money' set revealed at New York Stock Exchange
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: David Konrad, Managing Director of Equity Research at KBW, joins Worldwide Exchange to discuss bank earnings ahead of Bank of America and Goldman Sachs reports this morning.
item.title: David Konrad: The financials trade has been largely oversold
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Claire Atkinson, Insider chief media correspondent, and Matthew Belloni, Puck founding partner, join 'Squawk Box' to discuss Disney's C-Suite clash.
item.title: Insider's Claire Atkinson and Puck's Matt Belloni break down the C-Suite clash at Disney
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Greg Hayes, CEO of Raytheon Technologies, joins Worldwide Exchange to preview the Farnborough Air Show.
item.title: Exclusive: Raytheon CEO Greg Hayes says supply chain issues will persist into 2023 or 2024
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Joyce Chang, JPMorgan chair of global research, and Maneesh Deshpande, Barclays head of U.S. equity strategy, join 'Squawk on the Street' to discuss what the drawdown on commodity prices mean for markets, the peak concern for global markets and more.
item.title: Europe remains a key concern for global markets, says Barclays' Desphande
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Ben Emons, Managing Director of Global Macro Strategy at Medley Global Advisors, joins Worldwide Exchange to discuss expectations for Fed rate hikes, the inflation picture, and emerging markets.
item.title: Ben Emons: The strong dollar could put a strain on emerging markets
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Sara Fischer, Axios media reporter, joins 'Squawk Box' joins to discuss Elon Musk's ongoing lawsuit with Twitter.
item.title: Elon Musk-Twitter saga: A look at possible outcomes
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: CNBC's Leslie Picker joins 'Squawk Box' to report Goldman Sachs earnings beat.
item.title: Goldman Sachs earnings beat second-quarter estimates amid strong bond trading
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Bill Nolen, FAA acting administrator, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss airline cancellations, summer travel demand, and the airline industry.
item.title: Airlines need to make sure their networks meet customer demands, says FAA's Bill Nolen
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: CNBC's Phil LeBeau joins 'Squawk Box' with Akbar Al Baker, Qatar CEO, to discuss the company, the airline industry, and his ongoing lawsuit with Airbus.
item.title: Boeing is a mightier aircraft maker than Airbus, says Qatar CEO Akbar Al Baker
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Jay Clayton, former U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission chair, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss stock trading in congress, ESG, and the energy crisis.
item.title: There shouldn't be an all-out ban on stock trading in Congress, says former SEC Chair Jay Clayton
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: David Katz of Matrix Asset Advisors believes stocks will be significantly higher a year from now, and highlights names in the financial, consumer discretionary and medical products sectors.
item.title: Katz: The hope is the market takes earnings season in stride, and looks to the next 6-12 months
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Amos Hochstein, Presidential energy adviser, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss takeaways from Biden's Saudi Arabia trip and the energy sector.
item.title: Presidential energy adviser Amos Hochstein breaks down Biden's Saudi trip
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Jim Cramer, Mad Money host, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss his first take on Mad Money's move to NYSE and big banks' earning reports.
item.title: Jim Cramer gives his take on Big Bank earnings
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: CNBC's Leslie Picker joins 'Squawk Box' to report Bank of America earnings.
item.title: Bank of America revenue beats forecasts, boosted by higher interest rates
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: David Calhoun, Boeing CEO, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss Delta ordering 100 Boeing planes and on 737 max production.
item.title: Boeing CEO on supply chain disruptions: Engine constraints will last about 18 months
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Watch the opening bell for the Nasdaq and the New York Stock Exchange from July 18, 2022.
item.title: Opening Bell, July 18, 2022
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Kari Firestone, Aureus Asset Management chairman and CEO, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss big bank earnings and what it means for the markets.
item.title: If bank earnings maintain, then the markets can maintain a rally, says Aureus' Kari Firestone
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: CNBC's Phil LeBeau joins 'Squawk Box' with John Plueger, Air Lease CEO, to discuss airline industry shortages, supply chain disruptions, and costs.
item.title: Airplane shortages will last a couple of years, says Air Lease CEO John Plueger
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: ‘Mad Money’ host Jim Cramer and the ‘Squawk on the Street’ team break down the Twitter-Musk lawsuit's latest developments.
item.title: Cramer on Elon Musk's Twitter defense: Are you kidding? Is that the best you have?
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Helima Croft, head of global commodity strategy at RBC Capital Markets, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss oil and energy and Biden's trip to Saudi Arabia.
item.title: We need Saudi oil going to Europe, says RBC Capital's Helima Croft
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Kristina Partsinevelos joins 'Squawk Box' to report on extreme heat in the south with possible blackouts.
item.title: Heat wave grips American South, putting strain on Texas power grid
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Roger Ferguson, a distinguished fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss big bank earnings, the Fed, and inflation.
item.title: Fed will have to do more to get demand down, says Roger Ferguson
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: The Squawk Box team discusses Apple's request to a court to throw out an injunction allowing developers to bypass Apple's app store payment system. And Alphabet completes a 20-for-1 split.
item.title: Executive Edge: Apple urges court to throw out app store injunction
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Bjarne Schieldrop, chief commodities analyst at SEB, says Russia is a major linchpin in the global energy markets, as it's the largest exporter of oil and natural gas in the world.
item.title: SEB: We need to remember we have an energy crisis overall globally beyond oil
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Tony Capuano, Marriott CEO, joins 'Squawk on the Street' to discuss where Capuano sees the opportunity for Marriott going, when foreign travelers into the U.S. gets back to pre-pandemic levels and how the luxury business will perform amidst current market volatility.
item.title: Higher hotel prices look sustainable if we deliver on the service, says Marriott CEO
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: ‘Mad Money’ host Jim Cramer and the ‘Squawk on the Street’ team break down bank earnings.
item.title: Jim Cramer: The consumer is strong; it will be difficult for the Fed to slow economy
item.author: CNBC Television

item.description: Neal Dingmann, Truist Securities, joins 'Squawk on the Street' to discuss his thoughts around oil and natural gas, how much higher said prices could go and what will drive companies to have less capital discipline.
item.title: Oil and natural gas prices will go higher due to lack of supply, says Truist's Dingmann
item.author: CNBC Television
